meta.subject: Travel and Tourism
meta.philosophy: The Travel and Tourism Syllabus has been developed by the need to provide a national curriculum for Zambia that is aligned with the Competence-Based Curriculum and 21st Century Skills. It aims to impart the knowledge, skills, values and positive attitudes that are aimed at enabling children to live and grow into productive and useful members of their communities and the Zambian society.
meta.competence_learning: The O Level Travel and Tourism syllabus, which is Competence-Based, encourages learners to appreciate the scale and scope of the Travel and Tourism industry in Zambia and the SADC region, thus recognizing the positive and negative impacts the industry may have on host communities and economies. Learners will discover that the Travel and Tourism industry is dynamic and how the industry responds to changing consumer needs and expectations.
goals: Enable learners locate tourism regions and analyse tourism destination and attractions in Zambia.
Enable learners plan tourism destination packages and assess carrying capacity.
Enable learners demonstrate skills used by intermediaries in the Tourism Industry.
Enable learners plan events and tours according to seasons.
Enable learners manage the impacts of tourism.

subtopics: 2.1.1. Tourism destination and attractions in Zambia

specific_outcomes: 2.2.2.1. Assess the carrying capacity of tourism destinations

subtopics: 2.3.1. Intermediaries in the Tourism industry - Travel Agents and Tour Operators
specific_outcomes: 2.3.1.1. Demonstrate skills used by intermediaries in Tourism industry
2.4.1.1. Plan events and tours according to seasons
